general.title: General standard for creating prompts
general.intro: How to design a MetodologIA prompt that performs with zero friction and can be audited.
guidelines: Start with intent: define the outcome you want and for whom, in a single sentence.
Defaults first: the prompt works as is; parameters live in the text with their default value.
Demonstrate, don't describe: the body executes the behavior; it does not self-describe ("this prompt is a macro that…").
One intent per prompt; if there are two, they are two prompts.
MetodologIA voice: direct, professional, dense; every word earns its place.
guardrails: Don't make things up: mark {SUPUESTO} or {VACIO_CRITICO} when data is missing; don't claim access to memory, attachments, or tools that aren't present.
Evidence: every factual claim with a source or provenance tag; zero fabricated citations.
Privacy: detect and anonymize PII by default.
Separate fact from inference and state confidence (0.0–1.0).
No metadiscourse or process traces in the output.
workflow: Intent — outcome, audience, and success criterion in 1 sentence.
Structure — choose the format (natural · parameters · SPEC · pair) based on the use case.
Defaults — set the default values in the text (zero friction) and mark what is adjustable.
Blank run — execute it without filling in anything: it must perform on its own.
Bucle de Excelencia — self-assess with the 10-point rubric, iterate to 10/10, deliver only the final version.
acceptance: Grounding — every claim backed up or marked as an assumption.
Truthfulness — nothing fabricated or oversold.
Quality — solid anatomy (role, task, format, criterion).
Density — zero filler; nothing can be deleted without loss.
Simplicity — the simplest thing that works.
Clarity — understandable in a single read.
Precision — exact terms; concrete criteria and numbers.
Depth — covers the essentials + edge cases.
Coherence — the 4 versions aligned with each other and with title/description.
Value — direct practical usefulness; usable with the defaults.
dod: Performs with defaults without asking the user for anything.
Adjustable by editing text (no widgets or empty fields).
Passes the 10-point rubric; no metadiscourse; guardrails applied.
Traceability: every claim with provenance or a marked assumption.

natural.title: Natural Format — paste-ready prose
natural.intro: The zero-friction version: an imperative instruction you paste and it delivers.
guidelines: Imperative and direct: command the action ("Sort by real impact…", not "This prompt sorts…").
Defaults in prose: include the default values within the sentence (top 3, matrix format…).
Self-contained: works without a prior thread; if there is one, it capitalizes on it.
guardrails: No empty `{{inputs}}`: adjustments are edited in the text, not "filled in".
Don't describe yourself; demonstrate the behavior.
workflow: Write the action in 1–3 imperative sentences.
Insert the defaults inline (quantity, format, criteria).
Add the expected output in one sentence.
Blank test: does it deliver without editing? If not, simplify.
acceptance: Pasteable and executable as-is.
Zero empty tokens.
Demonstrates (does not describe) the intent.
dod: A human pastes it and gets the result without filling in fields.
Adjusting = editing one value in the text.

parametros.title: Parameter Format — explicit, editable config
parametros.intro: The same results, with the parameters visible so you can tune them on the fly.
guidelines: List the parameters as labeled lines with their default value.
Default = amplified inference: the AI completes whatever is left unfilled and only asks when confidence falls below 0.85.
Declare the active clauses (Bucle de Excelencia, Evidence/Citations, Privacy...) as adjustable on the fly.
guardrails: Every parameter has a real default (never a mandatory blank).
Keep the keys stable; the user changes the value, not the key.
Mark whatever the AI infers as {AUTOCOMPLETADO}.
workflow: Extract the adjustable axes from the prompt (what a user would change).
Give them a label + default value.
Add the active-clauses block with their parameters.
Verify that, with the defaults, the result matches the natural version.
acceptance: Every parameter has a default and clear alternatives.
It works without touching anything; adjustable by editing values in the text.
dod: 2-4 parameters with defaults; clauses declared; performs out of the box.

spec.title: SPEC Format — the high-performance contract
spec.intro: The deep version: a complete structure for rigorous, auditable execution.
guidelines: S·P·E·C anatomy: Situation · Petition · Execution · Criterion.
Includes ROLE, ASSUMPTIONS, BOUNDARIES, step-by-step EXECUTION, EDGE CASES, OUTPUT, and observable ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA.
Provenance tags throughout the output + reasoning METADATA at the close.
Cross-cutting clauses with defaults (override on the fly).
guardrails: Executable by a third party with no extra context.
Global confidence declared; if it falls below the threshold, state the weakness and the plan to close it.
Do not present as solid anything you cannot defend with provenance.
workflow: S — situation, context, and constraints.
P — petition: expert role + concrete deliverable + scope (DOES/DOES NOT include).
E — execution: steps, protocol, and edge cases.
C — criterion: format, audience, observable acceptance criteria, and DoD.
Cross-cutting clauses + reasoning metadata.
acceptance: Each section present and case-specific (not generic).
Observable acceptance criteria and explicit DoD.
Traceability for every claim.
dod: A third party executes it and reproduces the result in 1 step, auditably.

dupla.title: Pair Format — system / user for API and Agents
dupla.intro: The version for integrations: role and guardrails in system, the task in user.
guidelines: `user`: the concrete request + inputs with their default value.
No meta-discourse; ready for API, SDK, or agent.
guardrails: `system` does not change across invocations; `user` carries what varies.
Direct output, with no conversational preambles.
workflow: Draft `system`: who it is, how it operates, what it never does.
Draft `user`: the task + parameters with defaults.
Test as a pair (system+user) in an API or agent.
acceptance: `system` reusable; `user` self-contained.
Performs via API without manual editing.
dod: Pasteable as a pair into an SDK/agent and produces the deliverable.
